title: Fort Bed
summary: A low kids' bed with solid particle-board end panels — one closed, one open with built-in climbing rungs — and slatted sides that read like fort walls.
description:
The two ends are built as two different panels on purpose: the closed end is one tall solid particle-board panel, while the open end is a shorter panel with its own 2x2 base and a set of ladder rungs built right into the frame — so a kid climbs in over the low end rather than needing a separate ladder leaned against the bed. Both ends get their 1x2 trim glued and screwed to the particle board as what the plan calls a permanent joint, since this isn't a seam that ever needs to come apart again.

The slats are the one genuinely fiddly part, and the plan is upfront that there's no single right way to attach them: screw straight into the ends of the slats and live with visible screw heads on the outside, add a 1x2 nailer on edge along the rails, pocket-hole them in if you have the jig, or rest them on steel angle brackets screwed to the inside of the rails. Pick based on what tools you already own — none of the four options changes how the finished bed performs.

tools.note: One of four options for attaching the slats.

steps.title: Build the open end panel
steps.body: Fasten the panel trim pieces to the open end panel board with glue and screws — this is a permanent joint. Then attach the legs and the bottom 2x2 to the legs with 3" screws and glue, predrilling your screw holes.

steps.title: Build the closed end panel
steps.body: Build the closed end the same way as the open end: glue and screw the trim boards to the particle board with 1-1/4" screws, then add the legs with 3" screws and glue, keeping the outside edges flush.

steps.title: Attach the side rail and bottom boards
steps.body: Using 3" screws, attach the side panel full-length boards between the two end panels to form the bed's rails and bottom framing.

steps.title: Add the ladder rungs and guardrails
steps.body: Attach the ladder rungs across the open end's legs to form climbing steps, and add the ladder panel guardrail boards alongside for safety.

steps.title: Attach the slats
steps.body: Choose one method for the slats: screw straight from the outside of the rails into the slat ends (visible screw heads); glue and screw a 1x2 nailer on edge to the rails first and attach slats to that; drill pocket holes if you have a jig and fasten to the rails that way; or screw steel angle brackets to the inside of the rails, rest the slats on top, and drive a couple of screws up from underneath.

steps.title: Reinforce and finish
steps.body: For extra rigidity, add corner braces at the leg joints. Sand every surface and apply finish.

images.alt: Kids' fort bed with a solid closed end and a ladder-rung open end
